column1: # text = Mother of 6-year-old with microcephaly: \"It gets better\" Like most six-year-olds, Edmund Picciuto loves singing, toys and, of course, his mother.
# text = But unlike most of his peers, Edmund has microcephaly, the result of a genetic disorder his mom Elizabeth Picciuto didn't know about while pregnant.
# text = When he first came home my initial thought was, I can't do this.
# text = I didn't sign up for this.
# text = Which is not true at all -- of course you sign up for it,\" she said.
# text = She and her husband, Vincent, were told Edmund might never recognize them, and that he might not even survive.
# text = Some doctors went as far as suggesting he be institutionalized.
# text = \"My first response was 'I didn't think people did that anymore,'\" Picciuto explained.
# text = Edmund walks with the help of a walker.
# text = Edmund didn't sit up or crawl until he was three, and he still doesn't talk.
# text = But he's slowly meeting some milestones.
# text = He's walking with help, and riding a bike.
# text = He also loves playing with his two brothers, and singing.
# text = Elizabeth demonstrates the sign language Edmund made up for \"please sing to me.\"
# text = \"We're teaching him sign language,\" Picciuto told CBS News.
# text = But it's not just typical sign language -- she says Edmund has invented some of his own signs.
# text = Microcephaly can arise from a number of different conditions: genetic ones like Edmund's, and infections during pregnancy from viruses like measles and Zika.
# text = Symptoms and prognosis can vary widely.
# text = It's too early to tell how it will affect the lives of those infected with Zika.
# text = Picciuto remembers the emotional toll of an uncertain future.
# text = I kept saying, what's going to happen?
# text = And she still doesn't really know.
# text = I actually haven't asked for a prognosis in forever, because I don't expect anyone to really be able to tell me.
# text = But Picciuto has hopeful advice for the mothers of babies with birth defects from Zika.
# text = You know it will get better for you.
# text = You will love your child, and your child will love you.
